Title: Clean in-network claim adjudicates to Paid
Preconditions: Member M-1001 active; Provider P-2001 active; Procedure 99213 covered
Steps: 1. Log in as Claims QA user. 2. Create professional claim CLM-5001 with service date 2026-03-15. 3. Submit claim. 4. Open claim detail screen.
Expected Result: Claim status is Paid; line status is Paid; allowed amount and paid amount display; no denial reason appears.

Title: Claim detail matches stored claim header
Preconditions: Claim CLM-5001 exists in Paid status
Steps: 1. Search for CLM-5001. 2. Compare UI status and amounts to expected values. 3. Run SQL validation.
Expected Result: UI claim header values match database claim header values.

Title: Inactive member claim denies
Preconditions: Member M-1002 inactive before service date; Provider P-2001 active
Steps: 1. Submit claim CLM-5002 for inactive member. 2. Open claim detail. 3. Review denial reason.
Expected Result: Claim status is Denied; paid amount is zero; denial reason is MEMBER_INACTIVE.

Title: Duplicate claim candidate is not paid
Preconditions: Existing claim CLM-5001 already paid; duplicate candidate CLM-5003 has same member provider service date procedure and amount
Steps: 1. Submit duplicate claim CLM-5003. 2. Review status. 3. Check duplicate flag.
Expected Result: Claim is flagged as duplicate candidate and is not automatically paid.

Title: SOAP claim status response matches database
Preconditions: Claim CLM-5001 exists in Paid status
Steps: 1. Open SOAP UI. 2. Submit claim-status request for CLM-5001. 3. Compare response to database.
Expected Result: SOAP response returns Paid status and matching allowed and paid amounts.

Title: Paid claim creates remittance record
Steps: 1. Confirm claim is paid. 2. Query payment and remittance tables. 3. Review 835-style output.
Expected Result: Payment row exists and remittance ID is populated.

Title: Denied line requires denial reason
Steps: 1. Open denied claim. 2. Review each claim line. 3. Query denied lines.
Expected Result: Each denied line has denial reason code and user-visible denial reason where authorized.

Title: Paid amount cannot exceed allowed amount
Preconditions: Claim data set includes amount validation scenarios
Steps: 1. Submit claim with billed amount greater than allowed. 2. Review adjudication. 3. Run amount validation SQL.
Expected Result: Paid amount is never greater than allowed amount.

Title: Inactive provider contract triggers denial or review
Preconditions: Provider P-2002 contract ended before service date
Steps: 1. Submit claim CLM-5004 for inactive provider. 2. Review outcome. 3. Query provider contract dates.
Expected Result: Claim does not pay automatically and includes provider contract reason.

Title: Unauthorized role cannot view restricted member details
Preconditions: QA user and read-only support user exist
Steps: 1. Log in as read-only support user. 2. Search claim CLM-5001. 3. Open member panel.
Expected Result: Restricted member fields are masked or hidden according to role.

Title: Claim status history retains lifecycle
Preconditions: Claim CLM-5001 moved from Received to Adjudicated to Paid
Steps: 1. Open claim history. 2. Query status history. 3. Compare latest status.
Expected Result: History contains expected transitions and latest status equals claim header status.

Title: Malformed SOAP request returns controlled error
Preconditions: SOAP endpoint available in integration environment
Steps: 1. Submit malformed XML request. 2. Review response body and status. 3. Confirm no stack trace or PHI.
Expected Result: Service returns controlled validation error with no sensitive data leakage.

Title: Regression pack for paid denied suspended adjusted claims
Steps: 1. Execute paid claim. 2. Execute denied claim. 3. Execute suspended duplicate. 4. Execute adjusted claim. 5. Run backend checks.
Expected Result: Regression pack completes with no critical or high failures.
